slide_2_Idea_Problem_Solution_Value: Only platform integrating product supply and operational automation together; all competitors offer tools OR products separately, never both integrated.
Asset-light model with zero warehouses and zero inventory; suppliers ship directly to sellers or buyers, enabling rapid geographic scaling without capital.
One seller reaches hundreds of existing buyers in KakaoTalk rooms organically; built-in distribution completely eliminates per-buyer customer acquisition cost.
Sourcing Market provides curated daily product selections directly to sellers, completely eliminating independent sourcing and inventory management needs.
AI-powered CRM continuously analyzes purchase history, repurchase cycles, and identifies at-risk customers within each group-buy community automatically.
Automated order fulfillment and settlement through payment links embedded in chat rooms completely replaces manual Excel-based workflows and processes.
Group-buy sellers spend 2-3 hours daily on manual order aggregation, customer list management, and payment verification through KakaoTalk and Excel.
Existing e-commerce platforms like Cafe24 and Naver are designed for open marketplaces and fundamentally fail to address closed-community selling needs.
Sellers must source products independently from suppliers; franchise opportunities demand significant store buildout and high upfront capital fees.
slide_3_Market_1_3_TAM_SAM_SOM_+_Why_Now: KakaoTalk 50M+ users with proven group-buy behavior; Kakao's 오늘공구 pilot reached 45K participants and 250K sales, validating channel monetization.
Accenture projects global social commerce at $1.2T; Korea has 1.16M solo creator businesses and 6.13M micro-merchants — structural seller base.
Vietnam Zalo 77.8M users enable mini-app commerce; McKinsey confirms SEA social e-commerce momentum — local supply, settlement, and playbooks.
Seoul metro sellers with sourcing & automation needs
Online: 30K active KakaoTalk group-buy open chats × 200 members × $75/mo spend × 12mo ≈ $5.4B. Including Naver Band, mom cafes, Instagram DM group buys ≈ $7.5B
Offline: 500K self-employed retail stores nationwide, ~10% convertible (50K stores) × $22K avg monthly GMV × 12mo ≈ $13.5B
Online: 30% of 30K active chats with sourcing needs (10K rooms) × $6K monthly GMV × 12mo ≈ $720M
Offline: 5% early adopters of 50K convertible stores (2,500) × $22K monthly GMV × 12mo ≈ $660M
Influencers & mom cafe operators: 5,000 sellers × $3.8K monthly GMV × 12mo ≈ $228M
slide_4_Market_2_3_Competition: Creator links, group-buy tools, and DM automation; most direct seller competitor — requires proven sourcing differentiation.
Connects brands with sellers and automates group-buy operations; achieved $10.4M revenue in 2022 — direct market competitor.
Value commerce platform achieving profitability in 2025; demonstrates repeat-purchase economics can scale — consumer-facing model.
Seller-first value commerce platform with 251M buyers and 846K sellers; $5.6B IPO proves seller-first approach scales globally.
Supply and tools integrated into one platform create switching costs; competitors offer empty SaaS or franchise models but lack integrated sourcing.
Sourcing edge, seller workflow lock-in, repeat purchase data, and settlement trust represent moat potential — but all are unrealized assets currently.
Korea beachhead with KakaoTalk 50M+ users and 1.16M creator businesses offers strong positioning; crowded with Inpock, Wired Company, platforms.
slide_5_Market_3_3_GTM_Pricing_BM_Scale: 3% transaction fee on all payments processed through platform
Premium SaaS subscription $21-36/month for advanced features
Sourcing Market margin approximately 5% on supplied product GMV
Three revenue pillars: transaction fees from payments, sourcing margins, and SaaS premium subscriptions
One seller reaches 200+ buyers organically; 500 sellers reach approximately 100K total buyers at zero advertising cost
Stage 1: Secure offline stores and online sellers in Seoul metro area through direct outreach, partnerships, and community expansion.
Stage 2: Scale operations nationally and activate seller viral loop to drive organic customer acquisition growth and market penetration.
Stage 3: Expand platform internationally to Vietnam and China with adapted supply chains, payment systems, and localized operations.
One seller acquisition automatically brings hundreds of buyers with zero per-buyer customer acquisition cost through existing chat groups.
Asset-light operations with no warehouse or inventory; supplier-direct shipping enables rapid geographic expansion without capital constraints.
Top seller success stories create viral loop momentum; successful sellers become influential evangelists recruiting new cohorts organically.
slide_6_Team_1_4_Composition_&_Roles: CEO Youngmin is ex-PwC CPA with deep M&A advisory expertise who successfully founded and scaled Motnany Market to 40K MAU, 100K+ monthly transactions, and validated concept.
CTO Woongsup is ex-Applied Intuition engineer from Silicon Valley and MindsLab AI specialist with full-stack architecture expertise and extensive AI/ML system development experience.
CEO drives product development strategy, sourcing network expansion, seller relationship sales, primary fundraising leadership; CTO architects platform engineering and technical infrastructure.
CEO validated community commerce market through Motnany Market and is pivoting to Parabang with existing supplier network relationships, domain expertise, and operator credibility.
Ex-PwC CPA with M&A advisory background; led enterprise client projects.
Motnany Market founder achieving 40K MAU with 300+ farm suppliers.
Direct seller relationships, supplier sourcing network, and operator experience.
Applied Intuition Silicon Valley engineer with full-stack architecture.
MindsLab AI systems engineer with strong AI/ML implementation expertise.
Platform engineering architecture and advanced automation systems design.
slide_7_Team_2_4_Program_Trajectory: Youngmin and Woongsup first met during Antler KOR8 cohort formation process; CEO brought validated Motnany Market business model and supplier relationships.
Youngmin actively recruited Woongsup as CTO during the Weeks 1-3 recruitment phase; founders aligned on values, work style, and long-term vision.
Team has experienced frequent minor disagreements on research methodology and deck structure throughout bootcamp; resolved through open dialogue consistently.
CEO previously validated community commerce market segment with Motnany Market achieving 40K MAU, 300+ farm supplier network, and 100K+ monthly transactions.
Bootcamp Week 3-2 presentation received Gabriel score of 8 with conditional approval; strategy solid but requires transaction data proof before IC meetings.
Attended recent Group Office Hours session with Gabriel advisor; actively refined key metrics and proved unit economics using detailed seller data.
MVP platform was launched 7 days before this evaluation snapshot; successfully onboarded 6 paying sellers total including 2 offline merchants and 4 online sellers.
Summarizes the journey from program start through team formation and PMF validation, based on weekly founder retrospectives and program team observations
slide_8_Team_3_4_Evaluation: Founders met at Antler cohort. CEO brought validated model; CTO joined with clear mandate. Gabriel bootcamp score 8.
Shipped MVP, onboarded 6 paying sellers in 7 days. Motnany: 40K MAU, 100K+ transactions, 300+ farm suppliers.
Successfully pivoted from Motnany Market to Parabang. Difficult strategic decision made decisively. Limited examples.
CEO is customer and operator. Analyzed 30K community rooms. Detailed pricing and multi-channel GTM approach.
Tech stack: Applied Intuition (SV), MindsLab, LINE. Full-stack plus advanced AI/ML. MVP built.
Supply and tools integrated; market-differentiated. Asset-light versus China incumbents. Validated concept.

slide_9_Team_4_4_Strengths_&_Improvement: BCG, McKinsey, Accenture, and Bain confirm community-buy and social commerce as structural growth — not niche.
Motnany Market built 300+ farm network with public execution evidence — sourcing expertise directly maps to core product.
KakaoTalk 50M+ MAU with proven group-buy behavior; 1.16M solo creator businesses, 27.9% e-commerce ready.
Inpock, Wired Company, Kakao compete directly — fragmented market with multiple funded players already present.
Entire model depends on KakaoTalk group chats; Kakao tested group-buy directly — policy changes pose existential risk.
Sourcing, settlement, CS, and seller success are labor-heavy — without retention proof, becomes commerce operator not SaaS.

● Parabang was initiated by the CEO pre-Antler, with validated traction and core business assets already in place; the CTO joined later as a true partner contributing to both product and business, with the 80:20 split reflecting initial contribution differences rather than role importance
slide_10_Investor_Assessment: The community commerce market size is huge and growing at a rapid pace
Indirect competition exists in different forms including group-buying platforms, influencer group-buyings, offline supermarket franchise group-buying solutions, etc.
The team's solution seems to be most asset-light, convenient, and smart CRM for sellers and end consumers as well as its own bargaining power growing as the platform grows. So, highly defensible.
8m KRW over 3 days of MVP launch. Both online and offline sellers are getting easily onboarded without any friction and adopted the team's solution right way to sell products curated by the team.
Make sense to open the same but adjusted platform for Chinese sellers seeking for Korean-authentic products. Vietnam and SEA markets are also accessible with local sourcing partners.
Strong execution with domain expertise. CEO is strong in numbers which is especially important in commerce business. CTO fast in shipping quality outputs meeting dues even under high pressure.

Overall Summary : Strong and balanced team. Moat being established from sourcing capacity in house. Smart business idea that turns 100K+ sellers into B2B customers. Business model and economics get stronger as the number of sellers and following consumers grow. Right timing to pursue the business idea as ordinary e-commerce platforms are suffering from low margins and fierce competition as consumers are becoming more and more platform-agnostic when purchasing

Key Risks : Addition of COO to the team is needed to rapidly onboard offline supermarkets or businesses as sellers across the nation. The team is aware that they need one. If fails, their grow speed might not be fast enough allowing fast-follower with sourcing power can start capturing market share
